notes: Comprehensive review covering strength, muscle recovery, and cognitive function; regularly updated
Systematic review of 6 RCTs (281 individuals): creatine may improve short-term memory and intelligence in healthy adults
Systematic review (6 studies, 1542 participants): 83% reported positive creatine-cognition relationship in older adults; memory and attention benefits; evidence quality mixed
Meta-analysis (20 studies, 1093 older adults): creatine + exercise significantly improved 1RM strength and reduced body fat vs. exercise alone

JISSN narrative review 2025: creatine monohydrate safe and effective for older adults; benefits for lean mass, strength, functional ability, glucose kinetics, and memory; application to sarcopenia, osteoporosis, frailty
